card.intuition: Label the edge to the child with the largest subtree as 'heavy'; all other edges are 'light'. Every root-to-leaf path crosses at most O(log n) light edges, so the path decomposes into O(log n) contiguous chains — each chain can be backed by a segment tree for O(log n) path queries.
card.analogy: A highway system: the main road (heavy chain) runs through the biggest towns; smaller roads (light edges) branch off. Any journey uses at most O(log n) road changes — and each straight road segment is handled by one highway toll booth (segment tree).
card.time_complexity: O(n) decomposition; O(log² n) per path query/update with a segment tree per chain
when_to_use: Path sum / min / max queries on trees with updates
LCA queries in O(log n) without binary lifting tables
Any problem needing repeated range operations along tree paths
gotchas: Heavy child = child with the largest subtree size (break ties consistently)
Nodes on the same chain get consecutive positions in the flattened array — maintain a position[] array
When walking a path (u→v), always jump from the chain with the deeper head first

exercise.prompt: Compute subtree sizes and identify the heavy child of each node (the child with the largest subtree).
exercise.starter_code:
import sys
sys.setrecursionlimit(10000)

def build_hld(n, adj, root=0):
    """
    Returns:
      size[]  : subtree size for each node
      heavy[] : heavy child of each node (-1 if leaf)
    """
    size  = [1] * n
    heavy = [-1] * n

    def dfs(u, parent):
        max_size = 0
        for v in adj[u]:
            if v == parent:
                continue
            dfs(v, u)
            size[u] += size[v]
            if size[v] > max_size:
                max_size = size[v]
                heavy[u] = ___________   # heavy child is the one with largest subtree

    dfs(root, -1)
    return size, heavy
hints: Track max_size as you visit each child; when size[v] exceeds the current max, update both max_size and heavy[u] = v
exercise.test_code:
adj = [[] for _ in range(7)]
for u, v in [(0,1),(0,2),(1,3),(1,4),(4,5),(4,6)]:
    adj[u].append(v); adj[v].append(u)
size, heavy = build_hld(7, adj)
assert size[0] == 7
assert size[1] == 5  # subtree: 1,3,4,5,6
assert size[4] == 3  # subtree: 4,5,6
assert heavy[0] == 1  # node 1 has larger subtree than node 2
assert heavy[1] == 4  # node 4 has size 3; node 3 has size 1
assert heavy[4] in (5, 6)  # either child (both size 1, tie)
assert heavy[3] == -1  # leaf
print('PASS')
